heading: Sale of prize
label: 8865 Sale of prize
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7665.34 U.S.C. 1143R.S. 4627.In subsection (a) the word “perishing” is omitted as surplusage. The words “in value” are added after “depreciate” for clarity.In subsection (c) the words “An appeal does not prevent” are substituted for the words “no appeal shall operate to prevent”.
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7665 of this title

children.text: the court finds, at any stage of the proceedings, that the property is perishable, liable to deteriorate, or liable to depreciate in value; or
the cost of keeping the property is disproportionate to its value.

children.label: b
children.text: The court may order a sale of the prize property if, after the return-day on the libel, all the parties in interest who have appeared in the cause agree to it.

children.label: c
children.text: An appeal does not prevent the order of a sale under this section or the execution of such an order.
